seo.title: AI-Powered Search implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping AI-Powered Search projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist outlines the technical requirements for transitioning an AI-powered search implementation from a prototype to a production-ready system. It focuses on retrieval accuracy, system performance, and infrastructure reliability.

items.title: Idempotent Indexing Pipeline
items.description: Ensure the indexing script can be re-run on the same dataset without creating duplicate entries by using deterministic IDs based on content hashes.

items.title: Standardized Chunking Strategy
items.description: Implement a fixed chunking strategy (e.g., recursive character splitting) with a defined token overlap (e.g., 10-15%) to maintain context across boundaries.

items.title: Metadata Schema Validation
items.description: Enforce a strict schema for metadata (dates, categories, user permissions) to ensure filtering logic does not fail during vector retrieval.

items.title: Dead-Letter Queue for Failures
items.description: Configure a queue to capture documents that fail embedding generation or vector database insertion for manual review or automated retry.

items.title: Incremental Update Logic
items.description: Develop a mechanism to identify and index only new or modified documents rather than rebuilding the entire index on every update.

items.title: Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF)
items.description: Implement RRF to combine and normalize scores from keyword-based (BM25) and semantic (Vector) search results.

items.title: Hard Filter Implementation
items.description: Apply attribute-based filters (e.g., tenant_id, is_public) at the database level during the vector search to prevent data leakage.

items.title: Vector Similarity Thresholding
items.description: Define a minimum cosine similarity or Euclidean distance floor to prevent displaying irrelevant results for queries outside the index domain.

items.title: Query Expansion/Rewriting
items.description: Use an LLM to rewrite user queries into search-optimized terms or multiple variations to improve retrieval recall.

items.title: Stop-word and Noise Removal
items.description: Strip conversational filler from user queries before generating embeddings to reduce noise in the vector space.

items.title: Cross-Encoder Reranking
items.description: Integrate a reranking model (e.g., Cohere Rerank or BGE-Reranker) to process the top 20-50 retrieved candidates for final ordering.

items.title: Business Logic Boosting
items.description: Apply multiplicative boosts to search scores based on business metrics like document recency, popularity, or authority scores.

items.title: Diversity Re-ranking
items.description: Implement an algorithm (e.g., Maximal Marginal Relevance) to ensure the top results are not redundant variations of the same content.

items.title: Handling Multilingual Queries
items.description: Verify that the embedding model and reranker support the specific languages present in the production dataset.

items.title: Field-Level Weighting
items.description: Assign higher weights to matches found in titles or headers compared to matches found in the document body.

items.title: P99 Latency Benchmarking
items.description: Measure and document the 99th percentile response time for the full pipeline, including embedding, retrieval, and reranking.

items.title: Embedding Request Batching
items.description: Batch multiple text chunks into a single API call to the embedding provider to reduce network overhead during indexing.

items.title: Result Caching Strategy
items.description: Implement a cache (e.g., Redis) for the embeddings of the most frequent search queries to bypass LLM API latency.

items.title: Vector Index Quantization
items.description: Enable Product Quantization (PQ) or Scalar Quantization (SQ) in the vector DB to reduce memory usage and speed up search at scale.

items.title: Connection Pooling
items.description: Use connection pooling for the vector database to handle high concurrent search volumes without exhausting socket limits.

items.title: Golden Dataset Creation
items.description: Curate a set of 50+ query-document pairs to calculate NDCG and MRR metrics after every major algorithm or index change.

items.title: Zero-Result Logging
items.description: Log all queries that return no results or results below the similarity threshold to identify content gaps.

items.title: User Feedback Loop
items.description: Capture implicit (clicks) and explicit (thumbs up/down) feedback to create a training set for future model fine-tuning.

items.title: Embedding Drift Monitoring
items.description: Monitor the distribution of query embeddings over time to detect if user behavior is shifting away from the indexed content distribution.

items.title: Component-Level Tracing
items.description: Implement distributed tracing (e.g., OpenTelemetry) to isolate latency bottlenecks between the embedding API and the vector DB.

items.title: Source Attributions
items.description: Ensure every search result or AI summary includes clickable citations leading directly to the source document or chunk.

items.title: Optimistic UI Loaders
items.description: Show skeleton states for results immediately while the more expensive reranking or LLM summary steps complete.

items.title: Result Highlighting
items.description: Return and display the specific text snippets that triggered the match to provide immediate context to the user.

items.title: Error Graceful Degradation
items.description: Fallback to standard keyword search if the embedding API or vector database becomes unavailable.

items.title: Mobile Responsiveness
items.description: Test search result cards and filters on mobile viewports to ensure citations and metadata remain legible.
